line: All that you touch and all that you see is all your life will ever be.
The sun is the same in a relative way but you're older, shorter of breath and one day closer to death.
The child is grown, the dream is gone.
How I wish, how I wish you were here.
Money, it's a gas. Grab that cash with both hands and make a stash.
Run, rabbit run. Dig that hole, forget the sun.
Remember when you were young, you shone like the sun.

song: Another Brick in the Wall Pt. 2
line: We don't need no education. We don't need no thought control.
The grass was greener, the light was brighter.
Together we stand, divided we fall.
Strangers passing in the street, by chance two separate glances meet.
You have to be trusted by the people that you lie to.
The lunatic is on the grass.
And after all, we're only ordinary men.

song: The Great Gig in the Sky
line: And I am not frightened of dying. Any time will do, I don't mind.
Mother, do you think they'll drop the bomb?
Big man, pig man. Ha, ha, charade you are.
Harmlessly passing your time in the grassland away.
Tongue-tied and twisted, just an earth-bound misfit, I.
I knew the waiting had begun.
